Football league

Promotion League

The **Promotion League**, named the **Hoval Promotion League** for sponsorship reasons, is the third tier of the [Swiss football league system](/wiki/Swiss_football_league_system "Swiss football league system"). Eighteen clubs compete in the league, playing each other twice over course of the season. The champions are promoted to the second tier, the [Challenge League](/wiki/Challenge_League_(Switzerland) "Challenge League (Switzerland)"), while the bottom two teams are relegated to the [1st League Classic](/wiki/1._Liga_Classic "1. Liga Classic").

The league forms the semi-professional bridge to amateur football.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Overview

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Promotion_League&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Overview")]

Eighteen teams play each other twice, once home and once away, for a total of 34 games. The season begins in August and ends in May, interrupted through a winter break from late November to early March.

The team finishing in first place—subject to license requirements of professional football—will be promoted to the [division above](/wiki/Challenge_League_(Switzerland) "Challenge League (Switzerland)"), in turn the last-placed team of the second division will be relegated for the following season. Similarly, the bottom two teams are relegated to the [fourth tier](/wiki/1._Liga_Classic "1. Liga Classic") and replaced respectively.[[2]](#cite_note-Welt-2)

It is the highest league in Switzerland that permits participation of reserve teams.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Promotion_League&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History")]

The league was introduced as the *1. Liga Promotion* beginning with the 2012–13 season as part of a restructuring that saw the Challenge League reduced from 16 to 10 teams. This was done to decrease the competitive gap between it and the [Super League](/wiki/Swiss_Super_League "Swiss Super League") by converting it to a purely professional league. The Promotion League would therefore serve as the semi-professional link to amateur football.[[3]](#cite_note-3) The 1. Liga was consequently renamed to *1. Liga Classic*, before reverting again in 2013 when the former adopted its current name. For its maiden season, six teams were relegated from the Challenge League, while the rest joined from the division below.[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) In March 2020, the ongoing season was canceled after 17 games due to the [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic "COVID-19 pandemic") and the 2020–21 season suspended until further notice.[[6]](#cite_note-:0-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)

The league was expanded to 18 teams for the [2022–23 season](/wiki/2022-23_Swiss_Promotion_League "2022-23 Swiss Promotion League") and the number of allowed U21 teams is unrestricted.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

| [2016–17](/wiki/2016%E2%80%9317_Swiss_Promotion_League "2016–17 Swiss Promotion League") | [FC Rapperswil-Jona](/wiki/FC_Rapperswil-Jona "FC Rapperswil-Jona") |
| [2017–18](/wiki/2017%E2%80%9318_Swiss_Promotion_League "2017–18 Swiss Promotion League") | [SC Kriens](/wiki/SC_Kriens "SC Kriens") |
| [2018–19](/wiki/2018%E2%80%9319_Swiss_Promotion_League "2018–19 Swiss Promotion League") | [Stade Lausanne Ouchy](/wiki/FC_Stade_Lausanne_Ouchy "FC Stade Lausanne Ouchy") |
| [2019–20](/wiki/2019%E2%80%9320_Swiss_Promotion_League "2019–20 Swiss Promotion League") | None[[a]](#cite_note-a-10) |
| [2020–21](/wiki/2020%E2%80%9321_Swiss_Promotion_League "2020–21 Swiss Promotion League") | [Yverdon-Sport FC](/wiki/Yverdon-Sport_FC "Yverdon-Sport FC") |
| [2021–22](/wiki/2021%E2%80%9322_Swiss_Promotion_League "2021–22 Swiss Promotion League") | [AC Bellinzona](/wiki/AC_Bellinzona "AC Bellinzona")[[b]](#cite_note-b-11) |
| [2022–23](/wiki/2022-23_Swiss_Promotion_League "2022-23 Swiss Promotion League") | [FC Luzern](/wiki/FC_Luzern "FC Luzern") II[[c]](#cite_note-c-12) |
| [2023–24](/wiki/2023-24_Swiss_Promotion_League "2023-24 Swiss Promotion League") | [Étoile Carouge FC](/wiki/%C3%89toile_Carouge_FC "Étoile Carouge FC") |
| [2024–25](/wiki/2024%E2%80%9325_Promotion_League "2024–25 Promotion League") | FC Rapperswil-Jona |
| [2025–26](/wiki/2025%E2%80%9326_Promotion_League "2025–26 Promotion League") | TBD |

1. **[^](#cite_ref-a_10-0)** The 2019–20 season was canceled due to the [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic "COVID-19 pandemic").
2. **[^](#cite_ref-b_11-0)** Breitenrain was refused a license and withdrew its appeal alongside the license application. License holder and second-placed [AC Bellinzona](/wiki/AC_Bellinzona "AC Bellinzona") was promoted instead, but FC Breitenrain Bern was runner-up and AC Bellinzona crowned champions.
3. **[^](#cite_ref-c_12-0)** FC Luzern II was originally promotion but reserve team ineligible for promotion. License holder, second and third place [Stade Nyonnais](/wiki/FC_Stade_Nyonnais "FC Stade Nyonnais") and [FC Baden](/wiki/FC_Baden "FC Baden") was promoted instead.
